1	use harper_brill::UPOS;
2	use is_macro::Is;
3	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
4	
5	use crate::{
6	    DictWordMetadata, Number, Punctuation, Quote, TokenKind::Word, dict_word_metadata::Person,
7	};
8	
9	/// Generate wrapper code to pass a function call to the inner [`DictWordMetadata`],  
10	/// if the token is indeed a word, while also emitting method-level documentation.
11	macro_rules! delegate_to_metadata {
12	    ($($method:ident),* $(,)?) => {
13	        $(
14	            #[doc = concat!(
15	                "Delegates to [`DictWordMetadata::",
16	                stringify!($method),
17	                "`] when this token is a word.\n\n",
18	                "Returns `false` if the token is not a word."
19	            )]
20	            pub fn $method(&self) -> bool {
21	                let Word(Some(metadata)) = self else {
22	                    return false;
23	                };
24	                metadata.$method()
25	            }
26	        )*
27	    };
28	}
29	
30	/// The parsed value of a [`Token`](crate::Token).
31	/// Has a variety of queries available.
32	/// If there is a query missing, it may be easy to implement by just calling the
33	/// `delegate_to_metadata` macro.
34	#[derive(Debug, Is, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Default, PartialOrd, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
35	#[serde(tag = "kind", content = "value")]
36	pub enum TokenKind {
37	    /// `None` if the word does not exist in the dictionary.
38	    Word(Option<DictWordMetadata>),
39	    Punctuation(Punctuation),
40	    Decade,
41	    Number(Number),
42	    /// A sequence of " " spaces.
43	    Space(usize),
44	    /// A sequence of "\n" newlines
45	    Newline(usize),
46	    EmailAddress,
47	    Url,
48	    Hostname,
49	    /// A special token used for things like inline code blocks that should be
50	    /// ignored by all linters.
51	    #[default]
52	    Unlintable,
53	    ParagraphBreak,
54	    Regexish,
55	    HeadingStart,
56	}
57	
58	impl TokenKind {
59	    // DictWord metadata delegation methods grouped by part of speech
60	    delegate_to_metadata! {
61	        // Nominal methods (nouns and pronouns)
62	        is_nominal,
63	        is_noun,
64	        is_pronoun,
65	        is_proper_noun,
66	        is_singular_nominal,
67	        is_plural_nominal,
68	        is_possessive_nominal,
69	        is_non_plural_nominal,
70	        is_singular_noun,
71	        is_plural_noun,
72	        is_non_plural_noun,
73	        is_non_possessive_noun,
74	        is_countable_noun,
75	        is_non_countable_noun,
76	        is_mass_noun,
77	        is_mass_noun_only,
78	        is_non_mass_noun,
79	        is_singular_pronoun,
80	        is_plural_pronoun,
81	        is_non_plural_pronoun,
82	        is_reflexive_pronoun,
83	        is_personal_pronoun,
84	        is_first_person_singular_pronoun,
85	        is_first_person_plural_pronoun,
86	        is_second_person_pronoun,
87	        is_third_person_pronoun,
88	        is_third_person_singular_pronoun,
89	        is_third_person_plural_pronoun,
90	        is_subject_pronoun,
91	        is_object_pronoun,
92	        is_possessive_noun,
93	        // Note: possessive pronouns are: mine, ours, yours, his, hers, its, theirs
94	        is_possessive_pronoun,
95	
96	        // Verb methods
97	        is_verb,
98	        is_auxiliary_verb,
99	        is_linking_verb,
100	        is_verb_lemma,
101	        is_verb_past_form,
102	        is_verb_simple_past_form,
103	        is_verb_past_participle_form,
104	        is_verb_progressive_form,

7	use tower_lsp_server::lsp_types::{
8	    CodeAction, CodeActionKind, CodeActionOrCommand, Command, Diagnostic, NumberOrString, TextEdit,
9	    Uri, WorkspaceEdit,
10	};
11	
12	use crate::config::{CodeActionConfig, DiagnosticSeverity};
13	use crate::pos_conv::span_to_range;
14	
15	pub fn lints_to_diagnostics<'a>(
16	    source: &[char],
17	    lints: impl IntoIterator<Item = (&'a str, &'a [Lint])>,
18	    severity: DiagnosticSeverity,
19	) -> Vec<Diagnostic> {
20	    lints
21	        .into_iter()
22	        .flat_map(|(origin_tag, lints)| {
23	            lints
24	                .iter()
25	                .map(|lint| lint_to_diagnostic(lint, source, origin_tag, severity))
26	        })
27	        .collect()
28	}
29	
30	pub fn lint_to_code_actions<'a>(
31	    lint: &'a Lint,
32	    uri: &'a Uri,
33	    document: &Document,
34	    config: &CodeActionConfig,
35	) -> Vec<CodeActionOrCommand> {
36	    let mut results = Vec::new();
37	    let source = document.get_source();
38	
39	    results.extend(
40	        lint.suggestions
41	            .iter()
42	            .flat_map(|suggestion| {
43	                let range = span_to_range(source, lint.span);
44	
45	                let replace_string = match suggestion {
46	                    Suggestion::ReplaceWith(with) => with.iter().collect(),
47	                    Suggestion::Remove => "".to_string(),
48	                    Suggestion::InsertAfter(with) => {
49	                        format!("{}{}", lint.get_str(source), with.to_string())
50	                    }
51	                };
52	
53	                Some(CodeAction {
54	                    title: suggestion.to_string(),
55	                    kind: Some(CodeActionKind::QUICKFIX),
56	                    diagnostics: None,
57	                    edit: Some(WorkspaceEdit {
58	                        changes: Some(HashMap::from([(
59	                            uri.clone(),
60	                            vec![TextEdit {
61	                                range,
62	                                new_text: replace_string,
63	                            }],
64	                        )])),
65	                        document_changes: None,
66	                        change_annotations: None,
67	                    }),
68	                    command: Some(Command {
69	                        title: "Record lint statistic".to_owned(),
70	                        command: "HarperRecordLint".to_owned(),
71	                        arguments: Some(vec![Value::String(
72	                            serde_json::to_string(&RecordKind::from_lint(lint, document)).unwrap(),
73	                        )]),
74	                    }),
75	                    is_preferred: None,
76	                    disabled: None,
77	                    data: None,
78	                })
79	            })
80	            .map(CodeActionOrCommand::CodeAction),
81	    );
82	
83	    results.push(CodeActionOrCommand::Command(Command {
84	        title: "Ignore Harper error.".to_owned(),
85	        command: "HarperIgnoreLint".to_owned(),
86	        arguments: Some(vec![
87	            serde_json::Value::String(uri.to_string()),
88	            serde_json::to_value(lint).unwrap(),
89	        ]),
90	    }));
91	
92	    if lint.lint_kind.is_spelling() {
93	        let orig = lint.get_str(source);
94	
95	        results.push(CodeActionOrCommand::Command(Command::new(
96	            format!("Add \"{orig}\" to the user dictionary."),
97	            "HarperAddToUserDict".to_string(),
98	            Some(vec![orig.clone().into(), uri.to_string().into()]),
99	        )));
100	
101	        results.push(CodeActionOrCommand::Command(Command::new(
102	            format!("Add \"{orig}\" to the workspace dictionary."),
103	            "HarperAddToWSDict".to_string(),
104	            Some(vec![orig.clone().into(), uri.to_string().into()]),
105	        )));
106	
107	        results.push(CodeActionOrCommand::Command(Command::new(
108	            format!("Add \"{orig}\" to the file dictionary."),
109	            "HarperAddToFileDict".to_string(),
110	            Some(vec![orig.into(), uri.to_string().into()]),
111	        )));
112	    }
113	
114	    if config.force_stable {
115	        results.reverse();
116	    }
117	
118	    results
119	}
120	
121	fn lint_to_diagnostic(
122	    lint: &Lint,
123	    source: &[char],
124	    origin_tag: &str,
125	    severity: DiagnosticSeverity,
126	) -> Diagnostic {
127	    let range = span_to_range(source, lint.span);
128	
129	    Diagnostic {
130	        range,
131	        severity: Some(severity.to_lsp()),
132	        code_description: None,
133	        source: Some("Harper".to_owned()),
134	        code: Some(NumberOrString::String(origin_tag.to_string())),
135	        message: lint.message.clone(),
136	        related_information: None,
137	        tags: None,
138	        data: None,
139	    }
140	}
141

String:     /// A utility function that removes overlapping lints in a vector,
/// keeping the more important ones.
///
/// Note: this function will change the ordering of the lints.
pub fn remove_overlaps(lints: &mut Vec<Lint>) {
    if lints.len() < 2 {
        return;
    }

    let mut remove_indices = VecDeque::new();
    lints.sort_by_key(|l| l.priority);
    lints.sort_by_key(|l| (l.span.start, !0 - l.span.end));

    let mut cur = 0;

    for (i, lint) in lints.iter().enumerate() {
        if lint.span.start < cur {
            remove_indices.push_back(i);
            continue;
        }
        cur = lint.span.end;
    }

    lints.remove_indices(remove_indices);
}
